title: Design Trending / Top-K
tagline: A billion events a day, whiteboarded live
interviewPrompt: For today's Software Engineer interview, find the top 10 trending hashtags in real time.

beats.interviewer: For today's Software Engineer interview, find the top 10 trending hashtags out of a billion events a day, in real time.
beats.interviewee: Naive version first: count every hashtag in a big map, sort, and take the top 10.
beats.show: Naive: count everything in a map, sort, take the top 10.

beats.interviewee: That map doesn't fit in memory at a billion distinct keys, and sorting on every read is dead on arrival.
beats.show: A billion keys won't fit in memory; sorting per read is dead on arrival.

beats.interviewer: So what are you willing to give up?
beats.interviewee: Exactness. I need the top 10, approximately, right now. Once I'll accept approximate, the design opens up.
beats.show: Give up exactness: the top 10, approximate, right now.

beats.interviewee: A Count-Min Sketch, a fixed-size probabilistic counter that estimates frequency in kilobytes instead of gigabytes, paired with a small min-heap of the current top K. Stream the events off Kafka through the sketch and update the heap. Constant memory, constant update.
beats.show: Count-Min Sketch (KB) + a min-heap. Constant memory, constant update.

beats.interviewer: And if the business demanded exact counts?
beats.interviewee: Then you shard by hashtag and merge per-shard heaps, more infra for more accuracy. The sketch just trades a tiny over-count for fitting in RAM. Spotting that approximate is allowed is the thousand-x win.
beats.show: Exact = shard by hashtag + merge heaps. The sketch trades over-count for RAM.
